ground_truth.title: The Pros And Cons Of Package Tours: What Solo Travelers Should Know
ground_truth.author: Leyla
ground_truth.main_content:
If you're wavering between taking an organized tour or going solo on your next trip, read these pros and cons before you make up your mind. There are strong arguments on both sides of the fence. As an independent traveler I have tended to steer clear of organized tours. I was recently invited on an organized tour. I said No because of a scheduling conflict but in truth, I was relieved. The thought of cramming myself into a bus with a bunch of people I didn't know was scary. But it got me thinking: Why the reticence? Have I been shortchanging guided travel tours? After all, how can I judge something I don't know? So I decided to investigate. I read what I could, and I talked to women who love tours and women who dislike them intensely. Here's what I found out about the pros and cons of package tours. And it surprised me.

Discovery #1: There are definite advantages to tour packages. You don't have to lift a finger. Someone else will do your homework for you - booking the hotels, restaurants, transport from the airport and between cities, everything but the tips. You don't have to spend time with people you don't like. Your punctualities may not match. You can feel a bit rushed as you're whisked from A to B to C with no time to sit and soak in a locale. The pros and cons of package tours boil down to one thing: how well you choose your tour operator. There are different types of tour - and not all involve a bus. For years I railed against organized tours without knowing enough about them and just agreeing with what I heard. I've changed my mind - at least on certain types of tours, like walking or private or small group tours. I still slow travel or solo travel, but I'm no longer dead-set against group travel - if it's small.

Discovery #2: The disadvantages of packaged tours are equally obvious

items.alt: Tourists crowding around the Mona Lisa in the Louvre Museum, Paris
items.caption: There's a lot to be said for independent travel

items.alt: organized tours still allow you flexibility - like this isolated area of Sri Lanka
items.caption: I only had a week in Sri Lanka and having a guide made all the difference – I couldn't have seen half what I did otherwise

items.alt: Package tours can take you to off-the-beaten-path places - here, northern Senegal
items.caption: Here I am in northern Senegal whipping by on the bus trying to grab a snapshot – very unsatisfactory

items.alt: organized tours - eating during my Istanbul food tour
items.caption: Part of my multi-stop Istanbul food tour

items.alt: Moroccan organized tours - goats in an argan tree
items.caption: Sometimes having a guide allows you to see things you wouldn't otherwise see – like goats in argan trees in Morocco
